title: Friday Sermon 14 July 1995
description:
In the previous Friday Sermon I had drawn attention to preaching in connection with Allah Almighty's attribute of al-Haq [The Truth] and that to spread the truth is a natural outcome of one's connection with the True One.  In Sura al-Asr [The Age] the subject of 'urge to the truth' has been tied to 'urge to steadfastness'.*  Wherever the subject of preaching is mentioned one sees the subject of patience mentioned as well.  'The Holy Quran' does not mention the words Sabbar or Subur [The Patient] as attributes of Allah Almighty.  When I pondered over why Sabbar or Subur do not appear in 'The Holy Quran' as attributes of Almighty God it appeared that patience is faced with 2 trials of anger and of mercy and then there are 2 trials in relation to mercy.  Firstly to feel mercy towards a person and secondly to find oneself in a pitiful state.  And this second part cannot be related to the being of God in any way.  This is a subject which is related to mere anger that is to restrain oneself in a state of anger.   This subject is mentioned in 'The Holy Quran' in relation to the attribute of al Halim [The Mild].  This is a patience with regards to might and not in relation to weakness.  A person can be similar to Allah Almighty in relation to this type of patience.  If one has the power to retaliate and resists doing so for the sake of Allah's pleasure or for an ulterior motive then that is also regarded as patience.

The believers summon to Allah through Divine Grace.  Their recommendations do not have the slightest taint of anger in them and only the advice rendered through mercy and affection which succeeds.

The success of the preaching of the Holy Prophet ﷺ lay in its mercy and because his mercy was very extensive so he was also granted a large measure of patience.  It is patience which appeals to prayers and it is the prayer of the patient which is granted acceptance.  The prayer of the impatient is meaningless.  If the heart of a patient person comes out with a prayer that is fitting for a believer and if it includes Divine support then it destroys the enemy.  Patience is also vital to prayer.  A dream I had a long time ago carried the message that in my age Allah Almighty would grant us victory against the extensive and grand oppositions and that He would make us triumphant as a result of mercy and affection and love.

An astonishing spiritual transformation is taking place throughout the world and it will certainly take place in Pakistan too.  These clerics may do whatever they will because all their efforts will blow away as dust.  They will themselves be seized, they will become a sign of warning and God willing the majority of the nation will receive the guidance but we have yet to see some more days of torment and of testing our patience.  

Many of the Quranic verses also connect patience with prayer and if you keep being patient without recourse to prayer or are always patient without a personal relationship with God then this patience will be in vain.  Prayer should be given importance in every home for patience will be all the more fruitful as a result.

The act of patience also relates to virtue.  Those who are patience do good acts.  So be patience and act virtuously and continue to act virtuously with patience


* 'The Holy Quran'.  al-Asr [The Age]: 4.
